name: Venison Terrine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the venison and pork belly into 1-inch cubes.
2. Sauté shallots and garlic in rendered pork fat until wilted; let cool.
3. Combine meats, wine, gin, brandy, salt, pepper, juniper, thyme, rosemary, nutmeg, and bay leaf in a bowl. Cover tightly for at least 2 days (up to 1 week).
4. Remove the bay leaf from the meat mixture.
5. To reconstitute dried cranberries, place them in a small pan with 1/2 cup brandy. Bring to a boil, turn off heat, cover pot, and let steep until soft and all brandy absorbed (about 1/2 hour). Chop cranberries coarsely.
6. Grind the meat carefully; add chopped cranberries to the mixture after grinding.
7. Puree chicken livers with eggs in a food processor. Strain the mixture and add to the meat mixture. Mix vigorously until a cohesive mass forms; knead with hands, occasionally wetting fingers with cold water. The mixture is ready when it no longer sticks.
8. Line a 1-1/2 quart terrine with bacon slices. Place the meat mixture in the terrine, mounding about 1/4 inch above the top. Make a groove around the rim and add dried thyme along the line. Fold over the bacon, cover tightly with foil, and refrigerate.
9. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
10. Set the terrine in a roasting pan and pour boiling water halfway up the sides of the terrine. Bake until internal temperature reaches 155°F (68°C), about 2 hours. Remove from oven and press pâté to release juices.
11. Unwrap pâté, remove congealed fat or juices, and wrap well in plastic wrap or foil.
